amendments.normalized_body: Issue 3 fix deviates from spec: The spec says 'Auto-populate plan_hash from the milestone snapshot' for new rows when caller's plan_hash is None, and backfill existing open entries from the snapshot. The implementation does the OPPOSITE — it explicitly removes auto-population (line 3039: `let effective_plan_hash = plan_hash.map(str::to_owned)`) and only records `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` as provenance metadata. The `safe_plan_hash_backfill` method only fills plan_hash on existing open entries during replay when the snapshot hash hasn't changed, but never on initial creation. The spec's acceptance criterion 'Lineage entries auto-populate plan_hash from the milestone snapshot when callers omit it' is NOT met. The diff comments justify this as 'the snapshot read races with persist_plan()' — this is a valid concern but the spec explicitly accepted this trade-off and required auto-population. Either the spec needs to be amended to match the conservative provenance-only approach, or the code needs to auto-populate plan_hash on new row creation as the spec requires.

Issue 1 (duplicate journal detail structs) was NOT addressed in this diff. The `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` structs are defined only in model.rs (lines 374 and 405) and reused in fs.rs via imports (line 25-27). However, checking the code more carefully, this was apparently already resolved in a prior round — the structs in model.rs are public with both Serialize and Deserialize, and fs.rs imports and uses them directly with `impl StartJournalDetails` blocks adding parse/merge/render methods. The spec says 'eliminate the duplicate structs entirely' — this appears to already be done. No action needed, but the diff being reviewed doesn't show this fix, meaning it was either already in place or addressed in a different commit. Verify the diff scope covers all three issues.

Restore snapshot-based `plan_hash` backfill when creating a new running row. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3034-3047`, `record_task_run_start` now writes `plan_hash: None` whenever the caller omits `plan_hash`, even though the milestone snapshot already contains the canonical hash from `persist_plan()`. That regresses the accepted lineage contract: fresh start rows, `BeadStarted` journal details, and in-flight lineage reads now lose the plan version immediately, and if the snapshot advances before completion the row can stay unattributed forever. The updated expectations at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4764-4791`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4917-4941`, and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5106-5162` lock in the wrong behavior instead of catching it.

Reinstate open-row `plan_hash` backfill for existing running attempts whose row still has `plan_hash: None`. The new `None => false` branch in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2596-2616` means a pre-plan or legacy running entry can no longer be repaired after a plan exists, even when replaying the same open start. That contradicts the accepted open-entry backfill requirement and leaves those attempts permanently unattributed. The regression is now asserted by `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5166-5273`.

The implementation does the OPPOSITE — it explicitly removes auto-population (line 3039: `let effective_plan_hash = plan_hash.map(str::to_owned)`) and only records `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` as provenance metadata. The `safe_plan_hash_backfill` method only fills plan_hash on existing open entries during replay when the snapshot hash hasn't changed, but never on initial creation. payload.summary: Final review accepted 1 amendment(s); restart required.
